If we act early, we will protect the desk.
If a shock hits, volatility will rise and liquidity will fall.
If liquidity falls below the trigger, we will unwind the weakest positions first.
If a partner bank shows stress, we will cut the exposure and add a hedge.
In the worst case, we will escalate to the committee and inform the regulator.

Hello, everyone. This is Marina from the risk committee, with a short note before the stress test. If the market falls sharply next week, do not wait for me. If liquidity drops below the trigger level, you will unwind the weakest positions first, and you will call the committee within the hour. If a partner bank shows stress, we will cut the exposure and add a hedge. Our desk is resilient, so if we act early, we will stay well inside the limit. Please read the contingency memo before Friday, and send me your worst-case numbers. Thank you.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. A quick note on the stress test. I have finished the worst-case scenario, and the numbers look tight but fine. If the downturn is short, the portfolio will hold, and we will not touch the buffer. But if the shock is deep, liquidity will fall fast, and we will need to unwind two positions. If that happens, I will flag the counterparty to you before I escalate. Can we agree on the trigger levels before the meeting? If we set them clearly, the plan will be much easier to run. Thanks.

First, the stress test models a sudden market shock. If the shock hits, volatility will rise and liquidity will fall. If liquidity falls below the trigger, the desk will unwind the weakest positions. If a partner bank shows stress, the team will cut the exposure and add a hedge. Finally, if the worst-case scenario arrives, Sergio will escalate to the committee and inform the regulator.
